calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Feb-03-2020 Vacated

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff Dan Diaz To Attend Independent Medical Examination
Off Calendar Sep-10-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Stephanie Malla, Esq. of Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith T:415.362.2580, for defendant and moving party;
Shahryar Babdjouni, Esq. of Joseph Farzam Law Firm, T:310.226.6890, for plaintiff and opposing party, on Court Call.

Defendant's counsel stipulated to the Judge Pro Tem prior to the hearing; plaintiff's counsel stipulated to the Judge Pro Tem in the course of the hearing.

RULING: Defendant's counsel objected to plaintiff's counsel's failure to provide notice that he is appearing in today's hearing. The parties were allowed to argue & discuss sanctions imposed on plaintiff. After hearing, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on DEFENDANT ARMANDO BRITO's Motion To Compel Plaintiff Dan Diaz To Attend Independent Medical Examination, modified per agreement of parties, as follows:

Defendant's motion to compel plaintiff's attendance at a noticed medical examination is unopposed and granted. Plaintiff shall appear and permit examination completion on or before October 31, 2019 in Arizona or on such other date and time and different location to which counsel agree. If the plaintiff fails to appear for medical examination within said time frame there will be no further opportunity to do so and defendant may move for evidentiary sanctions based on violation of this Order. Plaintiff shall reimburse defendant in the sum of $1,800.00 for the costs incurred from his failure to appear at the previously scheduled medical examination. The court notes plaintiff made no attempt to advise his counsel or the examining physician of his last minute unavailability. The reimbursement shall be paid within forty-five days of service of a copy of this Order with Notice of Entry.

Order signed in open court.

Judge Pro Tem: Steven Stein; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.
